name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in formulation development, small molecule characterization, and degradation pathway analysis. Expertise in analytical techniques including FT-IR, UV-Vis, HPLC, and thermal analysis (TGA, DSC) for assessing formulation stability and degradation mechanisms. Strong background in structure-property relationships, process development, and method validation. Experienced in managing multiple projects and supporting cross-functional development teams.

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science

bullets: Conducted degradation pathway analysis for organic materials using thermal stress testing (TGA, DSC) and spectroscopic monitoring.
Characterized small molecules using FT-IR, UV-Vis, HPLC, and mass spectrometry to assess stability and purity.
Developed and optimized formulations and processes using DOE methodologies.

skills.Formulation Analysis: Degradation analysis, stability testing, small molecule characterization
skills.Analytical Methods: FT-IR, UV-Vis, HPLC, LC-MS, TGA, DSC, NMR
skills.Development Support: Method development, process development, DOE, quality control

projects.title: Bio-based Polymer Process Development
projects.description: Led chemical process development for novel bio-based polymer systems using statistical experimental design.

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Coatings Program
projects.description: Co-led process development for high-performance molecular thin-film coatings for defense optical applications.

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.
